title.en: Disagreement that made the relationship stronger

question.en: Tell me about a time you had a significant disagreement with a teammate where the relationship ended up STRONGER because of how you handled it. What did you say, and what did you NOT say?
question.es: Cuéntame sobre una ocasión en que tuviste un desacuerdo significativo con un compañero de equipo en la que la relación terminó siendo MÁS SÓLIDA gracias a cómo lo manejaste. ¿Qué dijiste, y qué fue lo que NO dijiste?

must_include: the specific words the candidate used in the hard part of the conversation — not paraphrased ('I said something like: I think you're right that the API is cleaner, but I'm worried about the migration cost we'd be handing to future teams' is the floor)
at least one thing the candidate consciously chose NOT to say — the omission is as diagnostic as what was said

a concrete sign the relationship was stronger after, not just fine — e.g., the other person explicitly sought their opinion later, the dynamic in meetings changed, the two worked differently on the next project
what the candidate actually conceded or changed their mind on — RFI disagreement stories rarely have a clean winner
the specific point of disagreement — what was being argued, not just that there was tension
must_avoid: 'I convinced them' as a punchline — if the story ends with the other person capitulating and the candidate's view prevailing in full, it is likely an overpowering story, not an RFI story
reducing the other person to a role or type ('the difficult PM', 'the junior dev who didn't get it') without naming them as a full actor with a real position
vague relationship-repair language without a specific signal ('we ended up having a great working relationship' with no evidence)
omitting what was actually at stake — both technically and interpersonally
